intro: Arachnorchis plicata, commonly known as the pleated greenhood orchid, is a species of terrestrial orchid native to Western Australia. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its health benefits, including its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Arachnorchis plicata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In Western herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tincture or tea, and is used to treat various conditions, including hypertension, anxiety, and gastrointestinal issues.
